heading: 9.7.15.6.2.3.Matrix Fragments for sparsewgmma.mma_async.m64nNk64
content:
A warpgroup executing sparse `wgmma.mma_async.m64nNk64` will compute an MMA operation of shape
`.m64nNk64` where N is a valid n dimension as listed in
[Matrix Shape](#asynchronous-warpgroup-level-matrix-shape).

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warpgroup so each thread of the
warpgroup holds a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A, from shared memory is documented in
  [Matrix Fragments for sparse wgmma.mma_async.m64nNk64](#asynchronous-warpgroup-level-matrix-fragment-sparse-wgmma-64n64).
* Multiplicand A, from registers:

  > | .atype | Fragments | Elements |
  > | --- | --- | --- |
  > | `.e4m3` /  `.e5m2` | A vector expression containing four `.b32`  registers, with each register containing four  non-zero `.e4m3` /`.e5m2` elements out of  eight consecutive elements from matrix A. | Non-zero elements:  a0, a1, a2, … , a15    Mapping of the non-zero  elements is as described in  [Sparse matrix storage](#asynchronous-warpgroup-level-sparse-matrix-storage) |
  > | `.s8` /  `.u8` | A vector expression containing four `.b32`  registers, with each register containing four  non-zero `.s8` /`.u8` elements out of  eight consecutive elements from matrix A. |
  >
  > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 179](#sparse-wgmma-64n64-e4m3-e5m2-s8-u8-a).
  >
  > 

[Figure: sparse-wgmma-64N64-e4m3-e5m2-s8-u8-A]
SPARSE WGMMA MATRIX A FRAGMENT LAYOUT
.m64nNk64 with .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .s8 / .u8 type

=============================================================
1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
=============================================================
Matrix: A (input matrix)
Dimensions: M=64, K=64 (the N dimension is variable, denoted nN)
This is a SPARSE layout, meaning the actual dense equivalent would be larger; the stored fragment represents the non-zero elements.
Thread indexing: tid % 128 (fragments repeat every 128 threads)

=============================================================
2. GRID STRUCTURE
=============================================================
Rows (R): Matrix rows 0 through 63
Columns (C): K-dimension indices 0 through 63, divided into 8 groups:
  C 0-7, C 8-15, C 16-23, C 24-31, C 32-39, C 40-47, C 48-55, C 56-63

The table header row shows: R\C | 0...7 | 8...15 | 16...23 | 24...31 | 32...39 | 40...47 | 48...55 | 56...63

=============================================================
3. COLOR-CODED THREAD GROUPS (by matrix row bands)
=============================================================
Blue band:   Matrix rows 0-15   -> threads T0-T31 (tid % 128 = 0-31)
Orange band: Matrix rows 16-31  -> threads T32-T63 (tid % 128 = 32-63)
Green band:  Matrix rows 32-47  -> threads T64-T95 (tid % 128 = 64-95)
Pink band:   Matrix rows 48-63  -> threads T96-T127 (tid % 128 = 96-127)

=============================================================
4. DETAILED ELEMENT ASSIGNMENTS PER THREAD
=============================================================

Each thread holds two sets of registers:
  - Left half (C columns 0-31):  registers containing {a0, a1, a2, a3} or {a4, a5, a6, a7}
  - Right half (C columns 32-63): registers containing {a8, a9, a10, a11} or {a12, a13, a14, a15}

--- BLUE BAND: Matrix rows 0-15, threads T0-T31 ---

Matrix rows 0-7 (even row group within blue band):
  C 0-7:    T0:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 8-15:   T1:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 16-23:  T2:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 24-31:  T3:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 32-39:  T0:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 40-47:  T1:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 48-55:  T2:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 56-63:  T3:{a8,a9,a10,a11}

Row 1 (matrix row 1):
  C 0-7:    T4:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 8-15:   T5:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 16-23:  T6:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 24-31:  T7:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 32-39:  T4:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 40-47:  T5:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 48-55:  T6:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 56-63:  T7:{a8,a9,a10,a11}

Rows 2-6 (matrix rows 2-6): pattern continues with T8-T27 in groups of 4, same element sets {a0,a1,a2,a3} left / {a8,a9,a10,a11} right

Row 7 (matrix row 7):
  C 0-7:    T28:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 8-15:   T29:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 16-23:  T30:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 24-31:  T31:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 32-39:  T28:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 40-47:  T29:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 48-55:  T30:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 56-63:  T31:{a8,a9,a10,a11}

Matrix rows 8-15 (odd row group within blue band):
Row 8:
  C 0-7:    T0:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 8-15:   T1:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 16-23:  T2:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 24-31:  T3:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 32-39:  T0:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 40-47:  T1:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 48-55:  T2:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 56-63:  T3:{a12,a13,a14,a15}

Rows 9-14: pattern continues with T4-T27 in groups of 4, same element sets {a4,a5,a6,a7} left / {a12,a13,a14,a15} right

Row 15:
  C 0-7:    T28:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 8-15:   T29:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 16-23:  T30:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 24-31:  T31:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 32-39:  T28:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 40-47:  T29:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 48-55:  T30:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 56-63:  T31:{a12,a13,a14,a15}

--- ORANGE BAND: Matrix rows 16-31, threads T32-T63 ---

Matrix rows 16-23 (even row group within orange band):
Row 16:
  C 0-7:    T32:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 8-15:   T33:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 16-23:  T34:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 24-31:  T35:{a0,a1,a2,a3}
  C 32-39:  T32:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 40-47:  T33:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 48-55:  T34:{a8,a9,a10,a11}
  C 56-63:  T35:{a8,a9,a10,a11}

Rows 17-22: pattern continues with T36-T59 in groups of 4, same element sets

Row 23 (shown as row 31 in figure due to labeling — the last row of the even sub-group):
  C 0-7:    T60:{a4,a5,a6,a7}   [NOTE: figure shows row 31 with T60-T63 holding {a4,a5,a6,a7}]
  C 8-15:   T61:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 16-23:  T62:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 24-31:  T63:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 32-39:  T60:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 40-47:  T61:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 48-55:  T62:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 56-63:  T63:{a12,a13,a14,a15}

Matrix rows 24-31 (odd row group within orange band):
Row 31 (last row of orange band):
  C 0-7:    T60:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 8-15:   T61:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 16-23:  T62:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 24-31:  T63:{a4,a5,a6,a7}
  C 32-39:  T60:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 40-47:  T61:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 48-55:  T62:{a12,a13,a14,a15}
  C 56-63:  T63:{a12,a13,a14,a15}

--- GREEN BAND: Matrix rows 32-47, threads T64-T95 ---

Matrix rows 32-39 (even row

  >
  >
  > Figure 179 Sparse WGMMA .m64nNk64 fragment layout for matrix A with `.e4m3`/ `.e5m2`/ `.s8`/ `.u8` type.
* Accumulator D:

  Matrix fragments for accumulator D are the same as in case of
  [Matrix Fragments for wgmma.mma_async.m64nNk32](#asynchronous-warpgroup-level-matrix-register-fragment-wgmma-64n32)
  for the same `.dtype` format.
* Multiplicand B:

  Shared memory layout for Matrix B is documented in
  [Matrix Fragments for sparse wgmma.mma_async.m64nNk64](#asynchronous-warpgroup-level-matrix-fragment-sparse-wgmma-64n64).
* Metadata operand is a `.b32` register containing 16 4-bit vectors each storing the indices of
  two non-zero elements of a 4-wide chunk of matrix A.

  [Figure 180](#sparse-wgmma-metadata-64n64-e4m3-e5m2-s8-u8-first32col) shows the mapping of the metadata
  bits to the elements of columns 0–31 of matrix A.

  > 

[Figure: sparse-mma-metadata-16864-u8s8-first32col]
SPARSE MMA METADATA LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
MMA Shape: m16n8k64
Data Types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1
Coverage: Columns 0 through 31 (first half of k=64 dimension)

TABLE STRUCTURE
The table has rows labeled 0–15 (matrix rows) and columns labeled by 4-wide column groups: 0..3, 4..7, 8..11, 12..15, 16..19, 20..23, 24..27, 28..31.

Each cell contains: Thread identifier (T_n) and bit range within that thread's metadata register, in the format T_n: [high..low].

FULL CELL-BY-CELL DATA

Row 0:
  Cols 0..3:   T0: [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   T0: [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  T0: [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: T0: [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: T0: [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: T0: [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: T0: [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: T0: [31..28]

Row 1:
  Cols 0..3:   T4: [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   T4: [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  T4: [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: T4: [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: T4: [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: T4: [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: T4: [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: T4: [31..28]

Row 2: (cells not explicitly labeled — implied continuation of pattern, indicated by diagonal arrow from row 1 col 28..31 toward row 7 col 0..3)

Row ... (rows 2–6 follow the same stride pattern, not individually labeled)

Row 7:
  Cols 0..3:   T28: [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   T28: [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  T28: [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: T28: [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: T28: [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: T28: [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: T28: [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: T28: [31..28]

Row 8:
  Cols 0..3:   T1: [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   T1: [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  T1: [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: T1: [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: T1: [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: T1: [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: T1: [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: T1: [31..28]

Row 9:
  Cols 0..3:   T5: [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   T5: [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  T5: [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: T5: [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: T5: [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: T5: [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: T5: [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: T5: [31..28]

Row 10: (cells not explicitly labeled — implied continuation, indicated by diagonal arrow from row 9 col 28..31 toward row 15 col 0..3)

Row ... (rows 10–14 follow the same stride pattern, not individually labeled)

Row 15:
  Cols 0..3:   T29: [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   T29: [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  T29: [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: T29: [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: T29: [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: T29: [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: T29: [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: T29: [31..28]

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN

Rows 0–7 use threads T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28 (stride of +4 per row).
  Row 0 -> T0
  Row 1 -> T4
  Row 2 -> T8
  Row 3 -> T12
  Row 4 -> T16
  Row 5 -> T20
  Row 6 -> T24
  Row 7 -> T28

Rows 8–15 use threads T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29 (stride of +4 per row, offset +1 from upper half).
  Row 8  -> T1
  Row 9  -> T5
  Row 10 -> T9
  Row 11 -> T13
  Row 12 -> T17
  Row 13 -> T21
  Row 14 -> T25
  Row 15 -> T29

BIT FIELD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN

Each thread holds a single 32-bit metadata register covering all 8 column groups (cols 0..31).
Each group of 4 columns consumes exactly 4 bits of the metadata register.
The bit fields are contiguous and non-overlapping across the 32-bit register:

  Cols 0..3:   bits [3..0]
  Cols 4..7:   bits [7..4]
  Cols 8..11:  bits [11..8]
  Cols 12..15: bits [15..12]
  Cols 16..19: bits [19..16]
  Cols 20..23: bits [23..20]
  Cols 24..27: bits [27..24]
  Cols 28..31: bits [31..28]

This means each 4-bit field encodes the sparsity selector for 4 consecutive columns within one matrix row. For 2:4 structured sparsity, each group of 4 elements contains exactly 2 non-zeros; 4 bits encode two 2-bit selectors (each 2-bit value selects which of the 4 positions is non-zero).

DIAGONAL ARROWS
Two diagonal arrows are shown in the figure:
1. An arrow from the row 1 / cols 28..31 cell (T4: [31..28]) pointing diagonally down-left toward row 7 / cols 0..3 (T28: [3..0]), indicating the continuation of the thread sequence T4 -> T8 -> ... -> T28 across rows 2–7.
2. An arrow from the row 9 / cols 28..31 cell (T5: [31..28]) pointing diagonally down-left toward row 15 / cols 0..3 (T29: [3..0]), indicating the continuation of the thread sequence T5 -> T9 -> ... -> T29 across rows 10–15.

SUMMARY OF KEY STRUCTURAL RULES
1. Each matrix row maps to exactly one thread.
2. Each thread's 32-bit register covers all 32 columns (cols 0..31) for that row.
3. Bit fields are 4 bits wide per 4-column group, packed LSB to MSB left to right.
4. Upper 8 rows (0–7) use even-indexed threads in multiples of 4: T0, T4, T8, T12, T16, T20, T24, T28.
5. Lower 8 rows (8–15) use odd-indexed threads offset by 1: T1, T5, T9, T13, T17, T21, T25, T29.
6. Threads T2, T3, T6, T7, T10, T11, etc. (those with index mod 4 = 2 or 3) are not used for this metadata layout in columns 0–31 (they likely cover columns 32–63, the second half of k=64).

  >
  >
  > Figure 180 Sparse WGMMA .m64nNk64 metadata layout for `.e4m3`/ `.e5m2`/ `.s8`/ `.u8` type for columns 0–31

  [Figure 181](#sparse-wgmma-metadata-64n64-e4m3-e5m2-s8-u8-last32col) shows the mapping of the metadata
  bits to the elements of columns 32–63 of matrix A.

  > 

[Figure: sparse-mma-metadata-16864-u8s8-last32col]
SPARSE MMA METADATA LAYOUT: .m16n8k64 for columns 32-63
Data types: .u8 / .s8 / .e4m3 / .e5m2 / .e3m2 / .e2m3 / .e2m1

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows the metadata layout for the second half (columns 32-63) of the m16n8k64 sparse MMA operation. The column groups are labeled in descending bit order (e.g., 35..32 means bits/columns 35 down to 32).

COLUMN GROUPINGS (8 groups of 4 columns each):
Column group 1: 35..32
Column group 2: 39..36
Column group 3: 43..40
Column group 4: 47..44
Column group 5: 51..48
Column group 6: 55..52
Column group 7: 59..56
Column group 8: 63..60

ROW-TO-THREAD-AND-BIT MAPPING (complete table):

Row 0:
  Cols 35..32:  T2:  [3..0]
  Cols 39..36:  T2:  [7..4]
  Cols 43..40:  T2:  [11..8]
  Cols 47..44:  T2:  [15..12]
  Cols 51..48:  T2:  [19..16]
  Cols 55..52:  T2:  [23..20]
  Cols 59..56:  T2:  [27..24]
  Cols 63..60:  T2:  [31..28]

Row 1:
  Cols 35..32:  T6:  [3..0]
  Cols 39..36:  T6:  [7..4]
  Cols 43..40:  T6:  [11..8]
  Cols 47..44:  T6:  [15..12]
  Cols 51..48:  T6:  [19..16]
  Cols 55..52:  T6:  [23..20]
  Cols 59..56:  T6:  [27..24]
  Cols 63..60:  T6:  [31..28]

Row 2: (empty cells — implied continuation of pattern, no explicit values shown)

Row 3 through 6: indicated by "..." (pattern continues)

Row 7:
  Cols 35..32:  T30: [3..0]
  Cols 39..36:  T30: [7..4]
  Cols 43..40:  T30: [11..8]
  Cols 47..44:  T30: [15..12]
  Cols 51..48:  T30: [19..16]
  Cols 55..52:  T30: [23..20]
  Cols 59..56:  T30: [27..24]
  Cols 63..60:  T30: [31..28]

Row 8:
  Cols 35..32:  T3:  [3..0]
  Cols 39..36:  T3:  [7..4]
  Cols 43..40:  T3:  [11..8]
  Cols 47..44:  T3:  [15..12]
  Cols 51..48:  T3:  [19..16]
  Cols 55..52:  T3:  [23..20]
  Cols 59..56:  T3:  [27..24]
  Cols 63..60:  T3:  [31..28]

Row 9:
  Cols 35..32:  T7:  [3..0]
  Cols 39..36:  T7:  [7..4]
  Cols 43..40:  T7:  [11..8]
  Cols 47..44:  T7:  [15..12]
  Cols 51..48:  T7:  [19..16]
  Cols 55..52:  T7:  [23..20]
  Cols 59..56:  T7:  [27..24]
  Cols 63..60:  T7:  [31..28]

Row 10: (empty cells — implied continuation of pattern)

Row 11 through 14: indicated by "..." (pattern continues)

Row 15:
  Cols 35..32:  T31: [3..0]
  Cols 39..36:  T31: [7..4]
  Cols 43..40:  T31: [11..8]
  Cols 47..44:  T31: [15..12]
  Cols 51..48:  T31: [19..16]
  Cols 55..52:  T31: [23..20]
  Cols 59..56:  T31: [27..24]
  Cols 63..60:  T31: [31..28]

THREAD PROGRESSION PATTERN:
Rows 0-7 use threads: T2, T6, ..., T30 (even-numbered threads starting at 2, incrementing by 4: T2, T6, T10, T14, T18, T22, T26, T30)
Rows 8-15 use threads: T3, T7, ..., T31 (odd-numbered threads starting at 3, incrementing by 4: T3, T7, T11, T15, T19, T23, T27, T31)

BIT ALLOCATION PER THREAD:
Each thread holds a 32-bit metadata register [31..0].
The 32 bits are divided into 8 groups of 4 bits each:
  Bits [3..0]   -> column group 35..32
  Bits [7..4]   -> column group 39..36
  Bits [11..8]  -> column group 43..40
  Bits [15..12] -> column group 47..44
  Bits [19..16] -> column group 51..48
  Bits [23..20] -> column group 55..52
  Bits [27..24] -> column group 59..56
  Bits [31..28] -> column group 63..60

ENCODING FORMAT:
Each 4-bit field encodes sparsity metadata for 4 consecutive columns. In 2:4 structured sparsity, 2 non-zero elements are selected out of every 4 elements. Each 4-bit group encodes which 2 of the 4 elements in that column group are non-zero (2 bits per selected element index).

DIAGONAL ARROWS:
Two diagonal arrows are visible in the figure, indicating the reading/continuation direction of the thread pattern across rows 2-7 (upper arrow) and rows 10-15 (lower arrow), showing that intermediate rows follow the same incremental thread numbering pattern between the explicitly shown boundary rows.

RELATIONSHIP TO COLUMNS 0-31:
This figure covers columns 32-63 only. The companion figure for columns 0-31 covers the first half of the k=64 dimension. Threads T2/T6/.../T30 and T3/T7/.../T31 each hold the full 32-bit metadata for both halves of their assigned rows, with bits [31..0] covering columns 32-63 as shown here.

  >
  >
  > Figure 181 Sparse WGMMA .m64nNk64 metadata layout for `.e4m3`/ `.e5m2`/ `.s8`/ `.u8` type for columns 32–63
